title: Hybrid Cross-Platform E2E Testing Harness

overview.description: Design and implement a comprehensive end-to-end testing harness using a hybrid approach: GitHub-hosted runners for macOS and Windows (cost-effective), plus AWS EC2 Spot instances for Linux distributions not available on GitHub (Fedora, Arch, Alpine, FreeBSD). This validates Jarvy tool installations across all supported platforms with real package managers in isolated, reproducible environments.
goals: True platform parity: Test on actual macOS (Intel + Apple Silicon), real Linux distros, Windows 10/11
Fresh environment guarantee: Every test runs on a clean, just-provisioned instance
Base tool validation: Verify a core set of tools installs correctly on each platform
Cost efficiency: Minimize AWS spend through spot instances, auto-termination, and smart scheduling
CI integration: Results flow back to GitHub PRs with clear pass/fail status
Reproducibility: Any failure can be reproduced by spinning up the same instance type

userStories.title: GitHub-Hosted Platform Testing
userStories.description: As a Jarvy maintainer, I want E2E tests to run on GitHub-hosted runners for macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows, so that I get free, reliable testing on the most common platforms.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: macOS tests run on both macos-13 (Intel) and macos-14 (Apple Silicon M1)
Ubuntu tests run on ubuntu-22.04 and ubuntu-24.04
Windows tests run on windows-latest with winget available
All GitHub-hosted jobs run in parallel via matrix strategy
Tests verify Jarvy builds and installs base tool set successfully

userStories.title: AWS EC2 Spot Testing for Additional Distros
userStories.description: As a Jarvy maintainer, I want E2E tests on Fedora, Arch, Alpine, and FreeBSD via AWS EC2 Spot instances, so that I can verify package manager support on distros not available on GitHub.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: EC2 Spot instances used for Fedora 40, Arch Linux, Alpine, and FreeBSD 14
Ephemeral self-hosted runners register, run job, then auto-terminate
Spot interruption handled gracefully (job marked as 'interrupted', not failed)
Cost per run < $0.05 for all 4 distros combined
Custom AMIs built with Packer include Rust toolchain pre-installed

userStories.title: Multi-Platform Parallel Execution
userStories.description: As a Jarvy maintainer, I want tests to run in parallel across all platforms, so that the full test suite completes in under 20 minutes.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GitHub Actions matrix runs all platforms concurrently
Individual platform failures don't block other platforms (fail-fast: false)
Results aggregation job waits for all matrix jobs
Total wall-clock time < 20 minutes for full 9-platform suite

userStories.title: Pre-Release Validation Gate
userStories.description: As a Jarvy maintainer, I want E2E tests to run as a pre-release validation gate, so that I can verify platform compatibility before shipping a release.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: E2E workflow triggers on release/pre-release creation
E2E workflow triggers on tags matching v* pattern
Manual workflow_dispatch available for pre-release validation
Release blocked if E2E tests fail (required status check)
Results visible in GitHub Actions summary with platform matrix

userStories.title: Test Result Artifacts
userStories.description: As a Jarvy maintainer, I want comprehensive test artifacts stored in GitHub Actions, so that I can debug failures without re-running tests.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Each platform job uploads artifacts with unique naming
Artifacts include results.json - Structured test results (tool, status, duration, error)
Artifacts include jarvy-output.log - Full stdout/stderr from jarvy commands
Artifacts include system-info.txt - OS version, arch, package manager versions
Artifacts retained for 30 days (GitHub default)
Artifacts downloadable directly from workflow run page

userStories.title: Scheduled Nightly Full Suite (Optional/Future)
userStories.description: As a Jarvy maintainer, I want the option to enable nightly E2E runs for regression detection between releases.
userStories.note: Deferred - E2E tests run only as pre-release checks. Nightly runs can be enabled later if needed by uncommenting schedule trigger in workflow.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Workflow includes commented-out schedule trigger for future use
Documentation explains how to enable nightly runs if desired

userStories.title: MacinCloud Fallback (Future)
userStories.description: As a Jarvy maintainer, I want the option to add MacinCloud as a macOS testing fallback, so that I can get cleaner environments if GitHub runners prove insufficient.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Architecture documented for MacinCloud integration
SSH-based self-hosted runner setup instructions provided
Cost comparison documented (GitHub free vs MacinCloud ~$20-50/mo)
Migration path from GitHub runners to MacinCloud defined

phases.title: GitHub-Native E2E (Week 1)
phases.description: Create the E2E test workflow using only GitHub-hosted runners

tasks.description: Create tests/e2e_base_tools.rs integration test
Create .github/workflows/e2e-cross-platform.yml with matrix strategy
Configure macOS (Intel + ARM), Ubuntu (22.04 + 24.04), Windows matrix
Implement result aggregation job with PR comments
Add release/tag trigger and workflow_dispatch for pre-release validation

deliverables: Working E2E tests on 5 platforms (all GitHub-hosted)

phases.title: AWS EC2 for Additional Distros (Week 2)
phases.description: Add EC2 Spot runners for Fedora, Arch, Alpine, and FreeBSD

tasks.description: Create Packer templates for 4 custom AMIs (Fedora 40, Arch Linux, Alpine, FreeBSD 14)
Create minimal Terraform for EC2 runner provisioning (infra/modules/ec2-runner/)
Configure self-hosted runner registration in user-data script
Add EC2-hosted jobs to GitHub workflow
Test Spot interruption handling

phases.title: Production Polish (Week 3)
phases.description: Harden the system and document everything

tasks.description: Add retry logic for flaky tests
Implement better error messages and debugging info
Create runbook for common issues
Document architecture and maintenance procedures
Set up AWS cost alerts ($5/month threshold)
Create AMI cleanup script (scripts/cleanup-old-amis.sh)
Configure smallest viable instance sizes (t3.small for most, t3.micro for Alpine)

deliverables: Reliable E2E suite with <5% flake rate

phases.title: MacinCloud Integration (Future - Optional)
phases.description: Only implement if GitHub macOS runners prove insufficient

tasks.description: Set up MacinCloud account and SSH access
Configure self-hosted runner on MacinCloud
Add to workflow matrix with conditional logic
Document cost trade-offs

nonGoals: Testing every tool (176 tools × 8 platforms = 1,408 combinations is not feasible)
Testing tool functionality beyond installation verification
Supporting cloud providers other than AWS (can be added later)

technicalConsiderations.details: Ephemeral runners terminate after single job, no persistent state. IAM least privilege. VPC isolation with NAT gateway for egress only. No secrets on runners.
GitHub-hosted runners are free. EC2 Spot instances cost ~$0.008/hr (80% savings vs on-demand). Ephemeral runners only run ~20 min per test. Total monthly cost estimate: $3-5.
Jobs on Spot instances may be interrupted. Handle gracefully by marking as 'interrupted' not failed. Only run EC2 tests on merge/nightly, not every PR.
Custom AMIs may drift over time. Schedule monthly AMI rebuilds with Packer. Pin package manager versions where possible.

risks.mitigation: Document known pre-installed tools; test 'install over existing'
Retry logic; jobs only run on merge/nightly, not every PR
Spread tests; use mirrors if needed
Ephemeral runners; isolated VPC; no secrets on instance

openQuestions: EC2 distro priority: Should we start with all 4 (Fedora, Arch, Alpine, FreeBSD) or prioritize?
PR vs main: Should EC2 tests run on PRs, or only on merge to main / nightly?
Failure policy: Should E2E failures block merges, or just be advisory?

external: Package manager mirrors (brew, apt, dnf, pacman, apk, winget, pkg)
